subject: Chemistry
solution:
An amine on reaction with benzenesulphonyl chloride produces a compound insoluble in alkaline solution. This amine can be prepared by ammonolysis of ethyl chloride.

It has to be 2° amine because on reaction with benzene sulphonylchloride it gives water in soluble product. As it is formed by ammonolysis of ethylchloride, so it has to be R-NH-Et type.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Vitamin helpful in the blood clotting is Vitamin K.

Vitamin K is a fat soluble vitamin required for normal blood clotting. Its deficiency leads to slow or delayed blood clotting and haemorrhage (blood loss). Vitamin K is required for synthesis of prothrombin in the liver where the vitamin K-dependent carboxylation in prothrombin synthesis occurs.

Note: Question has been slightly modified for its correctness.

Yb is the only element that does not form MO2 type oxide.

Ytterbium is a soft, malleable and rather ductile element that exhibits a bright silvery luster. A rare earth, the element is easily attacked and dissolved by mineral acids, slowly reacts with water, and oxidizes in air. The oxide forms a protective layer on the surface. Compounds of ytterbium are rare.

thus can be seprated of simple distillation.

Statement 2 : Mixture of aniline and water seprated by simple distillation.

A suitable method for separating a mixture of aniline and chloro form would be steam distillation. Steam distillation is the process used to separate aromatic compound from a mixture because of their temperature sensitivity. Therefore, steam distillation is an ideal method for their separation

subject: Chemistry
options: Carius tube is used in the estimation of sulphur in an organic compound.

Phosphoric acid produced on oxidation of phosphorus present in an organic compound is precipitated as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Mg</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>7</mn></msub></math> by adding magnesia mixture.
Carius method is used for the estimation of nitrogen in an organic compound.
Kjeldahl's method is used for the estimation of nitrogen in an organic compound.
solution:
Carius method is used in the estimation of halogen in organic compounds.

Carius method involves the conversion of the halogen present in the organic compound into the corresponding silver halide. From the mass of the organic compound taken and the mass of silver halide formed, the percentage of halogen in the compound can be calculated.
Sulphur present in any organic compound is also estimated by Carius method.

Sulfur dioxide gas turns acidified potassium dichromate (VI) solution from orange to green. Chromium is reduced from (VI) to (III) after reaction.

SO2+dil H2SO4⟶SO3g

SO3+K2Cr2O7→H2SO4dilCr2SO43

Hence, On treating a compound with warm dil. H2SO4, gas X is evolved which turns K2Cr2O7 paper acidified with dil. H2SO4 to a green compound Y. X and Y respectively are SO3 and Cr2(SO4)3.
